sections.paragraph:
τιμωρέω (τιμωρό ‘avenger’, fr. τιμή + a root or [as in the Dor. τιμάορος, Pind. O. 9, 84, but there in the sense of ‘paying honor to’]) 1 aor. ἐτιμώρησα Wsd 12:20; pf. 2 pl. τετιμωρήκατε (Tat. 27, 1). Mid.: fut. τιμωρήσομαι LXX; 1 aor. ἐτιμωρησάμην LXX. Pass.: 1 aor. ἐτιμωρήθην; pf. 3 sg. τετιμώρηται 4 Macc 18:5, ptc. τετιμωρημένος (TestSol 5:3) (Trag., Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, En; ParJer 7:26) punish τινά someone, have someone punished (Soph., Oed. Rex 107; Lysias 13, 41; 42; Jos., Ant. 2, 107; 7, 93; TestJos 14:1; Just.; Tat. 27, 1. But the mid. is much more widely used in this sense [it is customary in Philo; Jos., Ant. 1, 195; 17, 211]) Ac 26:11. τιμωρεῖν τινα δειναῖς τιμωρίαις punish someone with terrible punishments Hs 6, 3, 3. Pass. (X., An. 2, 5, 27; Pla., Gorg. 81, 525b; 2 Macc 7:7; En 22:13) Ac 22:5; Hs 6, 3, 4; 6, 5, 3f; 6.—DELG s.v. τιμωρό. M-M.

  1. fr. = from

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. pl. = plural

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. sg. = singular

ptc. ptc. = participle

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

En En = I Enoch—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1006
